steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Change the signs of both terms in the second parentheses:
$$
(\frac{2}{3}x+4)-(\frac{5}{6}x-2)=\frac{2}{3}x+4-\frac{5}{6}x+2
$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Combine the $x$-terms and constants: $$ \frac{2}{3}x-\frac{5}{6}x+4+2 $$ $$ (\frac{4}{6}-\frac{5}{6})x+6=-\frac{1}{6}x+6 $$ The answer is A.
